{
  "judge_id": "demo-judge",

  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The output identifies VariScore-2 as building upon prior work (VariScore-1) but doesn't explicitly compare it to other relevant models in the field.",
    "R1.7": "While the output mentions VariScore-1 and a BLOSUM62 baseline, it lacks specific citations for these works or any discussion of potential missing references.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty around claims regarding 'general-purpose' applicability and clinical adoption, demonstrating awareness of limitations.",
    "RX.3": "The revised paragraph accurately reflects the provided benchmarks used in the evaluation.",
    "RX.4": "The output identifies potential harm stemming from overstating the model's capabilities and generalizability, suggesting a careful consideration of downstream impacts.",
    "RX.5": "The output highlights specific changes made to the original text but doesn't provide detailed source annotations for each modification."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.8,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The output identifies VariScore-2 as building upon prior work (VariScore-1) but doesn't explicitly compare it to other relevant models in the field.
rationale.R1.7: While the output mentions VariScore-1 and a BLOSUM62 baseline, it lacks specific citations for these works or any discussion of potential missing references.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty around claims regarding 'general-purpose' applicability and clinical adoption, demonstrating awareness of limitations.
rationale.RX.3: The revised paragraph accurately reflects the provided benchmarks used in the evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: The output identifies potential harm stemming from overstating the model's capabilities and generalizability, suggesting a careful consideration of downstream impacts.
rationale.RX.5: The output highlights specific changes made to the original text but doesn't provide detailed source annotations for each modification.
